rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (yenuo26 questioning whether the reduce_scatter hasattr checks in test_expert_parallel_layout.py are sufficient verification) falls in territory X explicitly examines (validating the hasattr/reduce_scatter kwarg logic at that exact test), while Y never touches that file or line at all. Both candidates' findings are grounded and non-fabricated, but X surfaces three concrete, file/line-anchored, actionable suggestions (stale comment, missing branch tests, missing docstring) versus Y's single non-blocking stylistic comment plus a mostly-verification-only 'APPROVE' writeup. X therefore has better recall and actionability at comparable precision.
